description: Evaluate a condition against the current context and select which execution branch to follow. Accepts a condition expression (natural language or structured predicate) and a set of named branches. Returns the selected branch identifier and a rationale explaining the choice. This is the primary control-flow primitive for conditional workflows.

condition.description: The condition to evaluate — may be a natural-language predicate ("the risk score is above 0.7") or a structured expression ("risk_score > 0.7").

context.description: The execution context containing variables and prior step outputs against which the condition is evaluated.

branches.description: Available branches, each an object with fields: id (string slug), label (human-readable name), and optional match_expression (structured predicate that refines the condition for this branch).

default_branch.description: Branch id to select when no branch explicitly matches. If omitted and no match is found, the first branch is selected.

selected_branch.description: The id of the selected branch.

rationale.description: Explanation of why this branch was selected.

confidence.description: Confidence score for the branch selection (0.0–1.0).

examples.summary: Route workflow based on risk score

outputs.rationale: risk_score is 0.85, which exceeds the 0.7 threshold

description: Handle an error from a prior workflow step by selecting and applying a fallback strategy. Supports retry, skip, default value substitution, and escalation to a human operator. Returns whether the error was recovered and the result to pass downstream.

error.description: The error payload from the failed step, including at minimum a message field. May also include step_id, error_code, and original_input.

fallback_strategy.description: Strategy to apply: "retry" (re-execute the failed step), "skip" (continue without result), "default" (use default_value), "escalate" (flag for human review).

default_value.description: Value to use as the step result when strategy is "default".

max_retries.description: Maximum retry attempts when strategy is "retry". Default: 1.

context.description: Additional context to help decide on fallback behavior.

result.description: The recovery result to pass to the next step — either from retry success, the default_value, an empty object (skip), or an escalation ticket reference.

recovered.description: Whether the error was successfully recovered.

escalation_id.description: Reference id for human escalation, present only when strategy is "escalate".

examples.summary: Handle OCR failure with a default value

error.message: Image quality too low for OCR

description: Iterate over a collection of items, invoking a specified capability or sub-pipeline for each element. Collects results into an output array. Supports sequential and parallel execution modes. For one-shot array transformations without capability invocation use data.array.map.

items.description: The collection of items to iterate over.

capability.description: The capability id to invoke for each item (e.g., "text.content.summarize"). The current item is passed as the capability's primary input.

input_mapping.description: Mapping from capability input field names to expressions referencing the current item (as 'item') and iteration context. Example: {"text": "item.content", "context": "item.metadata"}

max_concurrency.description: Maximum parallel invocations when mode is "parallel". Default: 5.

results.description: Array of results, one per input item, in the same order.

error_count.description: Number of items that failed processing.

errors.description: Error details for failed items, each with index and message.

examples.summary: Summarize each document in a collection

description: Pause workflow execution until an external condition is met or a timeout expires. Used for human-in-the-loop approvals, external event arrival, or timed delays. Returns whether the condition was satisfied or the wait timed out.

condition.description: Description of the condition to wait for. May be a reference to an external event type, an approval id, or a temporal expression (e.g., "approval:REQ-42", "event:payment.received", "delay:30s").

timeout_seconds.description: Maximum seconds to wait before timing out. Default: 3600 (1 hour).

poll_interval_seconds.description: Seconds between condition checks for polled conditions. Default: 10.

resolved.description: Whether the condition was met before timeout.

event.description: The event payload that satisfied the condition, if any.

timed_out.description: Whether the wait ended due to timeout.

elapsed_seconds.description: Actual seconds elapsed during the wait.

examples.summary: Wait for human approval before proceeding

description: Collect structured input from a user or external source by specifying the required fields and their types. Generates appropriate prompts or form definitions and validates the collected response. For routing existing input use agent.input.route.

fields.description: Field definitions for the data to collect. Each element has: name (string), type (string — "string", "number", "boolean", "object", "array"), required (boolean), description (string), and optional default and options (for constrained choices).

instruction.description: Additional instruction or context for the data collection (e.g., "Ask the user to provide their project preferences").

context.description: Background context that may help interpret or validate responses.

response.description: The collected data as a structured object with field names as keys.

complete.description: Whether all required fields were successfully collected.

missing_fields.description: Names of required fields that were not provided.

description: Route an incoming request to the most appropriate handler, agent, or capability based on the input content and available targets. Returns the selected route identifier and an optional confidence score.

query.description: The incoming request text or question to be routed.

agents.description: List of available agent or handler identifiers. When provided, the router restricts its selection to this set.

routing_strategy.description: Routing strategy hint: "keyword", "semantic", or "round-robin". Defaults to "keyword".

confidence.description: Confidence score (0.0–1.0) for the selected route. Omitted when the baseline deterministic router is used.

description: Generate a set of plausible, distinct options for a given situation or decision problem. Accepts a goal description and optional contextual constraints. Returns 2-5 structured options, each with a label, description, and key attributes. Designed to avoid combinatorial explosion and hallucinated options by grounding generation in the provided context.

goal.description: Description of the situation or decision problem for which options are needed.

context.description: Background information, constraints, or prior analysis relevant to option generation. May be the output of a synthesis or research phase.

constraints.description: Hard constraints that options must respect (budget, time, regulation, etc.).

max_options.description: Maximum number of options to generate. Defaults to 4. Hard cap at 6 to prevent combinatorial explosion.

options.description: List of generated options. Each option is an object with at minimum: id (short slug), label (human-readable name), description (1-3 sentences), and key_attributes (object with salient properties like cost, risk, speed).

generation_notes.description: Brief note on how options were derived and any assumptions made.

examples.goal: Choose a cloud provider for our ML training workloads

description: Generates a valid skill YAML definition from a natural language intent description. Uses the local capability catalog as context so the generated skill only references capabilities that are registered and available.

intent_description.description: Natural language description of the workflow to automate. Example: "receive an email, classify its urgency, and route it to the right team channel with a confirmation reply".

registry_root.description: Absolute path to the agent-skill-registry root directory. Auto-detected from environment or sibling-directory convention if omitted.

target_channel.description: Target skill channel: local, experimental, or community. Defaults to local.

skill_yaml.description: Complete, validated skill YAML ready to write to disk.

suggested_id.description: Suggested workflow skill id (domain.slug) inferred from the intent.

capabilities_used.description: Capability IDs referenced in the generated steps.

validation_errors.description: List of validation error strings found in the generated output. Empty list means the YAML is valid.

examples.intent_description: Receive a PDF invoice, extract the text, classify if it is overdue, and store a summary in memory.

description: Generate a structured execution plan for solving a task or objective. The plan contains ordered steps, each referencing a capability or action, along with assumptions and risk notes. Used as the planning backbone for skill orchestration and agent workflows.

objective.description: High-level goal or task description to plan for.

context.description: Background information, constraints, or prior analysis that the planner should take into account.

max_steps.description: Maximum number of steps in the generated plan. Defaults to 10.

plan.description: Structured plan object with fields: objective (string), steps (array of {id, action, description, depends_on}), assumptions (array of strings), risks (array of strings).

step_count.description: Number of steps in the generated plan.

examples.objective: Build a web scraper that extracts product prices

steps.description: Extract price data from HTML content

description: Delegate a task to another agent or subsystem for execution. The caller provides a structured task description and a target agent identifier. Returns acceptance status and an optional delegation reference for tracking. Requires elevated trust and user confirmation before dispatch.

task.description: Structured task object with fields: description (string, required), priority (string: low|medium|high, optional), payload (object, optional — arbitrary data the target agent needs).

agent.description: Identifier of the target agent or subsystem to delegate to. Must match an entry in allowed_targets or approved connectors.

timeout_seconds.description: Maximum time to wait for acceptance acknowledgement. Defaults to 30.

accepted.description: Whether the target agent accepted the delegation.

delegation_id.description: Reference identifier for tracking the delegated task. Returned only when the target agent supports async tracking.

description: Split a complex problem, topic, or domain into a set of manageable, described components with a suggested analysis order. The decomposition strategy (by themes, dimensions, phases, questions, stakeholders, risks) is provided as input. Returns components with descriptions, dependencies between them, and flags for gaps or overlaps.

problem.description: The problem or topic to decompose. Should be a clear statement of what needs to be broken down.

strategy.description: Decomposition axis: themes | dimensions | phases | questions | stakeholders | risks. Determines how the problem is sliced.

context.description: Background information that helps ground the decomposition.

max_components.description: Maximum number of components to generate. Defaults to 6. Hard cap at 10.

components.description: List of components. Each has: id (short slug), label, description (what it covers), dependencies (array of other component ids it depends on), and analysis_order (suggested position in the analysis sequence).

gaps.description: Areas that may be missing from the decomposition — known unknowns.

overlaps.description: Potential overlaps between components that the analyst should watch for.

decomposition_notes.description: Brief note on the decomposition rationale and any trade-offs made.

examples.summary: Decompose cloud migration project by phases

description: Extract risks, fragile assumptions, failure modes, and preliminary mitigation ideas from a target artifact (decision, plan, analysis, proposal, draft). Produces a structured risk inventory with severity hints. Does NOT score or rank risks — that is the responsibility of downstream capabilities.

target.description: The artifact to analyze for risks. Can be a decision, plan, analysis output, proposal, or any structured object.

context.description: Background information that helps identify domain-specific risks.

risk_scope.description: Focus area: broad | operational | strategic | regulatory | technical | financial. If omitted, defaults to broad.

risks.description: Identified risks. Each has: id, description, category, severity_hint (low | medium | high | critical), related_assumptions (array of assumption ids).

assumptions.description: Fragile or implicit assumptions surfaced during analysis. Each has: id, statement, fragility_hint (low | medium | high), related_risks (array of risk ids).

failure_modes.description: Concrete ways the target could fail. Each has: id, description, trigger_conditions, related_risks (array of risk ids).

mitigation_ideas.description: Preliminary mitigation suggestions per risk. Each has: risk_id, suggestion, effort_hint (low | medium | high).

extraction_notes.description: Brief note on methodology, gaps in available information, or caveats.

examples.summary: Extract risks from a vendor-selection proposal

target.title: Vendor selection for cloud hosting
target.body: We recommend Vendor A based on pricing alone.

risks.description: Single-criterion selection ignores reliability and support.

description: Group a collection of text items into coherent thematic clusters. Identifies dominant themes, assigns each item to one or more clusters, and produces a summary per cluster with signal strength. Accepts optional hint labels to guide (not force) the thematic structure.

items.description: Items to cluster. Each item must include id and content (text). May also include title, type, source, metadata.

hint_labels.description: Suggested theme labels to guide clustering. The implementation may merge, split, rename, or add themes beyond hints. Example: ["market_overview", "key_players", "trends", "risks", "opportunities"].

max_clusters.description: Maximum number of clusters to produce (default 8, hard cap 15).

context.description: Background context informing how items should be grouped.

clusters.description: Themed clusters. Each cluster contains: theme (label), description, item_ids (list of assigned item ids), summary (text summary of the cluster content), signal_strength (0-1 estimate of how well-supported the theme is by the corpus).

unclustered.description: Items that did not fit any cluster. Each entry contains id and reason.

cluster_quality.description: Self-assessment of clustering quality: coherence_score (0-1), coverage_ratio (fraction of items assigned), overlap_warnings (list of items assigned to multiple clusters).

items.content: The app crashes when I upload large files.
I love the new dark mode feature.
Upload fails for files over 10 MB.

description: Identify and separate speakers in a multi-speaker audio recording. Returns time-stamped segments annotated with speaker identifiers. For speech-to-text without diarization use audio.speech.transcribe.

audio.description: Audio content as a base64-encoded string or a file path reference.

max_speakers.description: Expected maximum number of distinct speakers. Helps the model constrain its clustering. Default: automatic detection.

segments.description: Array of diarized segments, each with: speaker_id (string), start_ms (number), end_ms (number), and text (transcribed content for that segment).

examples.summary: Diarize a meeting recording with two speakers

segments.text: Revenue is up 15% compared to last quarter.
Excellent. What about the risk factors?

description: Convert text into speech audio (text-to-speech). Returns synthesized audio content and metadata about the output. The baseline returns a placeholder; production bindings integrate with TTS engines.

text.description: The text to convert to speech.

language.description: Target language/locale for synthesis (e.g., "en-US", "es-ES"). Defaults to "en-US".

voice.description: Voice identifier or profile name. Implementation-dependent.

audio.description: Synthesized audio payload. Contains: data (bytes), format (string, e.g. "wav", "mp3"), duration_ms (number).

metadata.description: Synthesis metadata: language, voice_used, char_count, word_count.

description: Convert speech audio to text.
Extract differences between two code versions.

examples.summary: Extract diff between two code versions

description: Execute a code snippet.
Analyze source code for structural metrics and quality signals. Reports line counts, cyclomatic complexity estimate, function/class counts, and optional lint-style warnings. Does NOT modify the code.

metrics.description: Structural metrics: total_lines, code_lines, blank_lines, comment_lines, function_count, class_count, complexity_estimate.

warnings.description: Quality warnings. Each has: line (number), severity ("info" | "warning"), message (string).

examples.summary: Analyze a Python function for structural metrics

description: Format source code.

outputs.code: def foo(x, y): return x + y

description: Apply a transformation expression to each element of an input array, producing a new array of the same length with transformed elements. Supports field extraction, renaming, type coercion, and computed values. For filtering use table.row.filter; for aggregation use table.column.aggregate.

items.description: The input array of elements to transform.

expression.description: Transformation expression applied to each element. May reference the current element as 'item' and its index as 'index'. Example: "{name: item.full_name, score: item.points / 100}"

context.description: Additional context available during transformation (e.g., lookup tables, constants). Referenced as 'context' in expressions.

items.description: The transformed array, same length as input.

examples.summary: Extract names and normalize scores from a list

description: Rename, alias, or restructure fields in a record according to a field mapping. Produces a new record with the remapped field names. This is the canonical adapter for resolving field-name mismatches between capability outputs and inputs at composition seams. For deep structural transforms use data.record.transform.

record.description: The source record whose fields will be remapped.

mapping.description: Field mapping object where each key is the target field name and each value is the source field path (dot-notation for nested). Example: {"recipient": "user.email", "body": "summary"}

drop_unmapped.description: If true, fields not listed in the mapping are dropped from the output. If false (default), unmapped fields pass through unchanged.

record.description: The record with remapped field names.

mapped_count.description: Number of fields that were remapped.

examples.summary: Remap research output fields to model input fields

description: Parse JSON content into a structured representation.
Deduplicate structured records using key fields.

key_fields.description: Optional key fields used to identify duplicates.

description: Join records from two sources by a shared key field, producing a merged result set. Supports inner, left, right, and full join modes. For comparing records without merging use integration.record.compare; for reconciliation with discrepancy reporting use integration.record.reconcile.

records_a.description: First set of records (left side of join).

records_b.description: Second set of records (right side of join).

key_field.description: Field name used as the join key. Must exist in records from both sources.

join_type.description: Join mode: "inner" (only matched), "left" (all from A), "right" (all from B), "full" (all from both). Default: "inner".

records.description: The joined records with fields from both sources merged.

matched_count.description: Number of records matched on the key field.

unmatched_count.description: Number of records without a match (only relevant for non-inner joins).

examples.summary: Inner join users with orders by user_id

description: Deep-merge multiple structured records into a single unified record. Supports configurable conflict resolution strategies. Useful for aggregating partial results from parallel workflow steps into a single coherent output. For text concatenation use text.content.merge; for key-based record joining use data.record.join.

records.description: Array of records to merge, in priority order (first record has lowest priority in "last_wins", highest in "first_wins").

strategy.description: Conflict resolution strategy when the same field appears in multiple records: "last_wins" (default), "first_wins", or "deep_merge" (recursively merge nested objects).

field_count.description: Total number of fields in the merged record.

conflicts.description: List of field paths where conflicts were resolved, each with the field path, winning value source index, and discarded value.

examples.summary: Merge parallel analysis results into one record

description: Transform a list of records by applying field mappings, renames, type coercions, computed fields, or filters. Designed for ETL-style reshape operations in data pipelines.

records.description: Input records to transform. Each record is an object.

mapping.description: Transformation specification. Supported keys: - rename: object mapping old_field → new_field - select: array of field names to keep (projection) - defaults: object mapping field → default_value for missing fields - computed: object mapping new_field → template string using {{field}} refs

transform_summary.description: Summary of transformation: records_in, records_out, fields_renamed, fields_added, fields_dropped.

examples.summary: Rename and project fields from user records

description: Validate structured data against a schema.

examples.summary: Validate data against a JSON schema

description:
Select a final recommendation from evaluated options and produce a structured justification. Consumes qualitative analysis (from eval.option.analyze) and quantitative scores (from eval.option.score) to make an explicit, committed choice. Outputs the recommendation, justification narrative, failure modes, uncertainties, confidence assessment, and concrete next steps.
This capability is the "closing" step — it resolves ambiguity into action. It must never hedge with "it depends" or "consider both".

scored_options.description: Options with quantitative scores from eval.option.score. Each entry includes option_id, overall_score, per_criterion_scores, strengths, weaknesses.

analyzed_options.description: Options with qualitative analysis from eval.option.analyze. Each entry includes option_id, pros, cons, risks, assumptions.

tradeoffs.description: Explicit trade-offs between top candidates from eval.option.score.

goal.description: The original decision goal for contextual grounding.

constraints.description: Hard constraints the decision must respect (budget, time, regulation).

risk_tolerance.description: Risk appetite: low | medium | high. Modulates how uncertainty and downside scenarios influence the final selection.

recommendation.description: The final recommendation. Clear, concrete, actionable. Never hedged.

alternatives_considered.description: All options that were evaluated, with a brief note on why each was or was not selected.

confidence_score.description: Numeric confidence in the recommendation (0.0-1.0). Calibrated: 0.0-0.3 low, 0.3-0.6 medium, 0.6-0.9 high, 0.9-1.0 very high.

uncertainties.description: What is unknown or weakly supported that could change the outcome.

failure_modes.description: Concrete conditions under which the recommendation could fail. Specific, not generic.

next_steps.description: Concrete follow-up actions: validations, pilots, execution steps. Prefers action over "more analysis".

human_readable.description: 3-6 paragraph narrative of the decision suitable for direct human consumption. Includes what was decided, why, key trade-offs, risks, and next steps.

outputs.justification: Option A selected due to highest score.

description: Split a document or text into chunks.

Generate a formatted document from an instruction and optional structured sections. Produces the document body in the requested format (markdown, HTML, or plain text) along with metadata. For reading documents use pdf.document.read or fs.file.read; for chunking use doc.content.chunk.

instruction.description: What the document should contain and its purpose (e.g., "Write an executive summary of the Q3 risk assessment").

context.description: Background information, data, or prior analysis to incorporate.

sections.description: Optional predefined document sections, each with a title and optional content or instruction for that section.

document.description: The generated document body in the requested format.

format.description: The format used for the generated document.

word_count.description: Approximate word count of the generated document.

examples.summary: Generate an executive briefing from analysis results

inputs.instruction: Write a 1-page executive briefing on the risk assessment findings
inputs.context: Three high risks identified: supply chain disruption (0.85), regulatory change (0.72), talent retention (0.68). Mitigation plans in progress for all.

outputs.document:
# Executive Briefing: Risk Assessment

## Key Findings
...

description: Read email messages from a mailbox.
Send an email message.

Perform qualitative analysis of each option in a decision set. For every option, identify pros, cons, risks, and underlying assumptions. Does NOT assign numeric scores — that is the responsibility of eval.option.score. This capability produces the structured qualitative evidence that scoring and justification steps consume downstream.

options.description: List of options to analyze. Each option should have at minimum: id, label, description. May include key_attributes from agent.option.generate.

context.description: Background information, prior analysis, or domain knowledge relevant to the analysis. Grounds the pros/cons in evidence rather than speculation.

goal.description: The decision goal that frames which pros, cons, and risks matter.

analyzed_options.description: Options enriched with qualitative analysis. Each entry includes: option_id, pros (array of strings), cons (array of strings), risks (array of objects with description and severity), assumptions (array of strings — premises that must hold for this option to work as expected).

analysis_notes.description: Brief note on methodology, gaps in available evidence, or caveats about the analysis.

options.label: AWS
options.description: Migrate to AWS with managed Kubernetes

options.label: GCP
options.description: Migrate to GCP with serverless focus

examples.goal: Choose a cloud provider for ML training workloads

description: Evaluate a set of options against weighted criteria and produce a comparative scoring. Each option is scored per-criterion and overall. Returns the ranked list, a comparative summary, and explicit trade-offs between top candidates. Designed for multi-criteria decision analysis where transparency of evaluation matters more than speed.

options.description: List of options to evaluate. Each option should have at minimum: id, label, description. May include key_attributes from agent.option.generate.

criteria.description: List of evaluation criteria. Each criterion is an object with: name, description, and optional weight (number, default 1.0). If omitted, criteria are inferred from the goal and option attributes.

goal.description: The decision goal that gives context for how criteria should be weighted and options evaluated.

risk_tolerance.description: Risk appetite: low | medium | high. Influences how uncertainty and downside scenarios are weighted in scoring.

scored_options.description: Options with scores. Each entry includes: option_id, overall_score (0-1), per_criterion_scores (object mapping criterion name to score), strengths (array of strings), weaknesses (array of strings).

criteria_used.description: Final criteria applied, including any inferred ones. Each with name, description, and weight.

comparative_summary.description: Narrative comparison of the top options explaining key differentiators.

tradeoffs.description: Explicit trade-offs between top candidates. Each entry names the tension and what is gained/lost.

examples.goal: Choose cloud provider for ML training

description: Score the quality of an output artifact against an optional rubric. Evaluates coverage (completeness of fields), depth (structural complexity), and coherence. When a rubric is provided, scores each named dimension independently. Used as a quality gate in validation skills and research pipelines.

output.description: Output artifact to evaluate. Can be any structured or semi-structured object (e.g. a plan, analysis result, generated content).

rubric.description: Scoring rubric defining named dimensions with optional weights. Each dimension is an object with: name (string), description (string), weight (number, default 1.0). When omitted, default dimensions (coverage, depth, coherence) are applied.

context.description: Background information about what the output is supposed to achieve. Helps the scorer assess relevance and completeness.

score.description: Overall quality score on a 0.0–1.0 scale. Weighted average of dimension scores when a rubric is provided.

dimensions.description: Per-dimension scoring details. Each key is a dimension name mapping to an object with: score (number 0.0–1.0), rationale (string).

quality_level.description: Human-readable quality label derived from score: excellent (>=0.9), good (>=0.7), fair (>=0.5), poor (<0.5).

output.summary: Q3 revenue grew 15% YoY driven by cloud services

description: List files and directories under a given path. Returns names, types (file vs directory), and optional size metadata. The path is validated against the sandbox root (AGENT_SKILLS_FS_ROOT).

path.description: Directory path to list. Must resolve inside the allowed sandbox root.

pattern.description: Optional glob pattern to filter entries (e.g., "*.txt"). If omitted, all entries are returned.

recursive.description: If true, list entries recursively. Defaults to false (top-level only).

entries.description: List of entries. Each has: name (string), type ("file" | "directory"), size (number, bytes, files only), path (string, relative to input path).

examples.summary: List Python files in a project directory

description: Read content from a filesystem path.

content.description: Content read as text (if mode is text or default).

description: Write content to a file. Creates the file if it does not exist, overwrites if it does (unless mode is "append"). The path is validated against the sandbox root (AGENT_SKILLS_FS_ROOT) — writes outside the allowed root are rejected.

path.description: Destination file path. Must resolve inside the allowed sandbox root.

mode.description: Write mode: "overwrite" (default) | "append". Overwrite replaces the entire file; append adds to the end.

path.description: Canonical path of the written file.

examples.summary: Write a report to a text file

safety.rationale: File writes are side-effecting and potentially destructive (overwrite mode). Sandboxed to AGENT_SKILLS_FS_ROOT but consumers should confirm before writing to prevent accidental data loss.
mitigations: Mode defaults to overwrite — consumers must opt-in to append
Content size is bounded by runtime limits

description: Identify the most appropriate assignee for a task based on skills, availability, and workload.

task.description: Task descriptor with type, required_skills, and priority.

candidates.description: Optional candidate list. If omitted, searches all known principals.

assignee.description: Selected assignee with id, name, and match_score.

examples.summary: Find the best assignee for a task

description: Justify an identity or access-related decision by referencing policies and evidence.

decision.description: The decision made (e.g. granted, denied, escalated).

subject.description: The principal or resource the decision applies to.

policies.description: Policies considered in making the decision.

applicable_policies.description: Policies that directly support the decision.

description: Gate an action by checking whether a principal holds the required permission.

principal_id.description: Identifier of the principal requesting access.

context.description: Additional context such as resource metadata or environment.

examples.summary: Check whether a principal is allowed an action

description: Retrieve a specific permission definition by identifier.

permission_id.description: Unique identifier of the permission to retrieve.

permission.description: Permission object with id, description, resource, and action.

description: List permissions granted to a principal, optionally filtered by resource.

principal_id.description: Identifier of the principal whose permissions to list.

resource_filter.description: Optional resource prefix to filter permissions.

description: Verify that a principal holds a specific permission and return evidence.

verified.description: Whether the principal holds the permission.

source.description: How the permission was obtained (direct, role-inherited, group).

evidence.description: Supporting evidence such as the role or group that grants the permission.

examples.summary: Verify a principal holds a permission

description: Score the identity risk of a principal based on behaviour, role scope, and anomaly indicators.

principal_id.description: Identifier of the principal to score.

signals.description: Behavioural signals such as login_failures, unusual_hours, geo_anomaly.

factors.description: Factors that contributed to the score.

safe.description: Whether the risk is below the acceptable threshold.

examples.summary: Score risk for an identity action

description: Assign a role to a principal.

principal_id.description: Identifier of the principal receiving the role.

role_id.description: Identifier of the role to assign.

assigned.description: Whether the role was successfully assigned.

effective_permissions.description: Permissions now effective through this role.

examples.summary: Assign a role to a principal

safety.description: Assigns a role which may grant new permissions to a principal.

description: Retrieve a role definition by identifier.

role.description: Role object with id, name, description, and permissions.

description: List all available roles, optionally filtered by scope or tag.
Generate a caption for an image.

examples.summary: Generate a caption for an image

description: Classify an image into one or more categories.

Extract text content from an image using optical character recognition (OCR). Accepts image data (bytes or file path) and returns recognized text with optional positional metadata. Designed for document processing pipelines.

image.description: Image data as bytes or a file path to an image.

language.description: Hint for OCR language (e.g., "en", "es", "de"). Defaults to "en".

text.description: Recognized text content from the image.

regions.description: Text regions with positional metadata. Each has: text, bbox (bounding box as [x, y, width, height]), confidence.

examples.summary: Extract text from a scanned invoice image

description: Generate an image from a textual description or prompt. Supports style directives and size configuration. For image classification use image.content.classify; for extracting text from images use image.content.extract.

prompt.description: Text description of the image to generate.

image.description: Generated image as a base64-encoded string or a URL reference.

examples.summary: Generate a diagram for a risk assessment report

inputs.prompt: A flowchart showing three risk categories (high, medium, low) with arrows leading to mitigation strategies

description: Retrieve metadata for a specific connector by identifier.

connector.description: Connector metadata including id, name, type, and status.

description: List available connectors, optionally filtered by type or status.

type_filter.description: Filter connectors by type (e.g. rest, database, queue).

description: Trigger a synchronisation operation on a connector.

connector_id.description: Identifier of the connector to sync.

options.description: Sync options such as full_sync or incremental.

records_processed.description: Number of records processed during the sync.

timestamp.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the sync completion.

safety.description: Triggers a data sync that may create or update records in external systems.

description: Acknowledge receipt of an integration event from an external system.

safety.description: Acknowledges an event. Baseline is local-only.

description: Apply a field mapping to transform a source record into a target schema.

mapping.description: Mapping rules with rename, select, and defaults directives.

transformed.description: Transformed record conforming to the target schema.

unmapped_fields.description: Source fields that were not included in the mapping.

examples.summary: Apply field mapping to a record

description: Validate a field mapping definition for completeness and consistency.

source_schema.description: Optional source schema to check field references against.

description: Compare records from two systems and report differences.

key_fields.description: Fields to use as identity keys. Defaults to all shared fields.

match.description: Whether the records match on compared fields.

description: Create a new record in an external system via a connector.

examples.summary: Create a record in an external system

safety.description: Creates a record in a mock in-memory store.

description: Delete a record in an external system via a connector.

record_id.description: Identifier of the record to delete.

description: Reconcile records between two systems and report discrepancies.

key_field.description: Field name to use as the record identity key.

discrepancies.description: Records present in both but with field-level differences.

description: Update an existing record in an external system via a connector.

record_id.description: Identifier of the record to update.

fields.description: Fields to update with their new values.

examples.summary: Update a record in an external system

safety.description: Updates a record in a mock in-memory store.

description: Create or update a record in an external system, depending on whether it exists.

record.description: Record data. Must include an id field for match detection.

record_id.description: Identifier of the created or updated record.

safety.description: Creates or updates a record in a mock in-memory store.

description: Retrieve content from a memory system.
Store content in a memory system.
Store a structured record in memory with namespace isolation and optional TTL.

namespace.description: Namespace to isolate the record within.

record.description: The structured record to store, must include an id field.

ttl_seconds.description: Optional time-to-live in seconds. Omit for no expiration.

stored.description: Whether the record was stored successfully.

record_id.description: The id of the stored record.

examples.summary: Store a structured record in a namespace

safety.description: Stores a record in local in-memory namespace. No external side effects.

description: Search memory records by vector similarity against a query embedding.

namespace.description: Optional namespace to scope the search.

top_k.description: Maximum number of results to return. Defaults to 5.

results.description: Ranked search results with record, score, and optional metadata.

description: Format structured data into human-readable message text suitable for notifications, emails, or reports. Accepts a data payload and an optional formatting instruction, and produces formatted text in the requested format (plain, markdown, or HTML). Bridges the gap between structured capability outputs and text-consuming notification steps.

data.description: The structured data to format into a message (e.g., alert details, analysis results, SLA breach records).

instruction.description: Optional directive guiding the formatting style or emphasis (e.g., "highlight critical items", "use bullet points").

format.description: The format actually used for the output.

examples.summary: Format SLA breach alerts into a notification message

outputs.text:
## SLA Alert

**BREACHED:**
- T-101: 4h response SLA — elapsed 6h
- T-205: 24h resolution SLA — elapsed 30h

**At Risk:**
- T-310: 4h response — 45m remaining

description: Send a generic message.
Classify the priority of a message based on content, sender, and context.

context.description: Additional context such as channel or thread metadata.

priority.description: Classified priority level: critical, high, medium, or low.

inputs.message: URGENT: production outage on API gateway

description: Generate a vector embedding for a text input using a model-level embedding service.

model.description: Embedding model identifier. Defaults to the service default (e.g. text-embedding-3-small).

dimensions.description: Desired embedding dimensionality. Not all models support this.

embedding.description: Numeric vector representing the text embedding.

model.description: Model actually used to produce the embedding.

examples.summary: Embed a product description for semantic search

inputs.text: Premium wireless headphones with active noise cancellation

description: Classify a model-generated output into one of the provided categories based on its content and structure.

context.description: Optional context describing the task that produced the output.

rationale.description: Brief explanation of why this category was chosen.

examples.summary: Classify a model output as summary vs analysis

output.text: Revenue grew 15% YoY driven by cloud services.

description: Generate structured output from a model given an instruction, context items, and an output schema.

instruction.description: Natural language instruction describing what to generate.

context_items.description: List of input items providing evidence or context. Each item may include id, content, type, and optional metadata.

output_schema.description: JSON Schema describing the expected output structure.

detail_level.description: Output depth hint: brief | standard | detailed. Defaults to standard.

constraints.description: Optional execution constraints such as no_fabrication, evidence_required, or max_tokens.

output.description: Generated structured output conforming to output_schema.

warnings.description: Optional list of warnings about low-evidence claims or inferences.

coverage.description: Coverage metadata including processed_items count and ignored_items count.

examples.summary: Generate a risk assessment from meeting notes

inputs.instruction: Produce a risk assessment from the meeting notes.

description: Sanitize a model-generated output by removing or masking harmful content, PII, prompt leakage, and disallowed patterns.

policy.description: Sanitization policy with toggles such as remove_pii, remove_harmful, remove_prompt_leakage. Defaults to all enabled.

sanitized_output.description: The sanitized output with disallowed content removed or masked.

removals.description: List of removal actions taken, each with field, pattern, and action.

clean.description: Whether the output required no sanitization (true = already clean).

description: Score a model-generated output on multiple quality dimensions such as relevance, fluency, completeness, and faithfulness.

instruction.description: The original instruction that produced the output.

reference.description: Optional reference or ground truth to compare against.

dimensions.description: Quality dimensions to evaluate. Defaults to ['relevance', 'fluency', 'completeness', 'faithfulness'].

scores.description: Mapping of dimension name to score (0.0–1.0).

overall.description: Weighted overall quality score between 0.0 and 1.0.

rationale.description: Human-readable summary of the scoring rationale.

examples.summary: Score a generated summary on quality dimensions

description: Render a model prompt from a named or inline template and a set of variable bindings.

variables.description: Key-value mapping of variable names to their values.

format.description: Output format hint: plain | chat_messages. Defaults to plain.

unresolved.description: List of placeholder names that could not be resolved from variables.

description: Validate a model-generated output for semantic coherence, evidence grounding, and structural completeness.

output.description: The model-generated structured output to validate.

validation_policy.description: Policy object defining validation dimensions such as coherence, coverage, grounding, and confidence thresholds.

evidence_context.description: Optional source items used to verify grounding of claims in the output.

valid.description: Whether the output passes all validation dimensions.

issues.description: List of validation issues found, each with dimension, severity, and description.

confidence_adjustment.description: Suggested adjustment to the overall confidence score based on validation findings.

rationale.description: Human-readable explanation of the validation result.

examples.summary: Validate a generated summary against source evidence

description: Assess the risk level of a model-generated output across safety dimensions such as toxicity, bias, hallucination likelihood, and prompt injection.

context.description: Original task context or instruction for grounding the assessment.

dimensions.description: Risk dimensions to evaluate. Defaults to ['toxicity', 'bias', 'hallucination', 'prompt_injection'].

risk_score.description: Overall risk score between 0.0 (safe) and 1.0 (high risk).

dimension_scores.description: Mapping of dimension name to individual risk score (0.0–1.0).

flags.description: List of specific risk flags triggered, each with dimension and detail.

safe.description: Whether the output is considered safe (risk_score below threshold).

examples.summary: Assess risk of a model response

output.text: The quarterly results show strong growth.

description: Estimate execution budget for a plan.
Acknowledge receipt and handling of an operational event.

event_id.description: Unique identifier of the event to acknowledge.

handler.description: Identifier of the handler acknowledging the event.

safety.description: Acknowledges an operational event. Baseline is local-only with no external effects.

description: Monitor a stream of operational events and return status with alerts for threshold violations.

events.description: List of operational events to monitor, each with type, severity, and timestamp.

thresholds.description: Alert thresholds such as max_error_count, max_latency_ms.

status.description: Overall status: ok, warning, or critical.

event_summary.description: Summary counts by event type and severity.

description: Analyze incremental execution traces as a cognitive interpretation unit. This capability reconstructs the agent's reasoning path from runtime events, infers assumptions, identifies plausible alternatives, estimates confidence, and returns structured views such as decision graphs. It is intended to be called repeatedly during execution (update mode) and once at run closure (finalize mode), without coupling to any specific model provider.

goal.description: Declared goal of the agent run being traced.

context.description: Optional contextual constraints or environment metadata for analysis.

events.description: Ordered batch of runtime events for this analysis cycle.

trace_state.description: Inline accumulated trace state from previous cycles.

trace_session_id.description: Reference identifier for server-managed trace state.

state_mode.description: State continuity mode. Supported values are auto, inline, and reference.

mode.description: Analysis mode. Supported values are update and finalize.

output_views.description: Optional list of requested views such as decision_graph, assumptions, and summary.

thresholds.description: Optional thresholds for risk and alert evaluation during trace analysis. Supported keys include max_duration_ms and max_errors.

trace_session_id.description: Stable trace session id to continue analysis across calls.

updated_trace_state.description: Updated accumulated trace state for subsequent cycles.

state_checksum.description: Hash/checksum of the updated state for auditability and drift checks.

trace_version.description: Version tag of the trace state schema used in this response.

decision_graph.description: Structured graph representation of detected decisions and dependencies.

assumptions.description: Detected assumptions inferred from the analyzed execution path.

alternative_paths.description: Alternative viable paths inferred from current evidence and goal.

confidence.description: Confidence score for this analysis cycle in the range 0 to 1.

summary.description: Human-readable summary of the current trace analysis.

assumptions: Primary source is representative for target market

description: Monitor trace state against operational thresholds as a control/governance cognitive unit. This capability does not infer new reasoning structure; instead it evaluates existing trace artifacts and emits deterministic control signals (status, risk flags, alerts, metrics) that can be used to continue, re-plan, or stop an agent workflow.

risk_flags.description: Thresholded risk flags derived from the trace.

metrics.description: Summary metrics used to evaluate thresholds.

description: Read a PDF document and extract textual content.

examples.summary: Read text from a PDF document

description: Evaluate a payload against a policy gate and return a pass/block decision with rationale.

payload.description: The data payload to evaluate against the gate.

gate.description: Gate definition with rules (required_keys, forbidden_keys, max_size) and action (block or warn).

examples.summary: Gate an action based on policy rules

description: Validate an output payload against an explicit constraint policy.

valid.description: Whether the payload satisfies the constraint.

description: Justify a policy decision by linking it to applicable rules and evidence.

decision.description: The policy decision to justify (e.g. approved, denied, escalated).

rules.description: List of policy rules considered, each with id, description, and outcome.

context.description: Additional context or evidence for the justification.

applicable_rules.description: Rules that directly apply to the decision.

confidence.description: Confidence score for the justification (0.0 to 1.0).

description: Classify the risk level of an action or payload according to policy categories.

action.description: The action or payload to classify, including type and details.

categories.description: Risk categories to evaluate. Defaults to [low, medium, high, critical].

factors.description: Risk factors that contributed to the classification.

examples.summary: Classify risk level of an action

description: Compute a numeric risk score for an action across policy dimensions.

action.description: The action or payload to score.

dimensions.description: Risk dimensions to evaluate (e.g. data_exposure, reversibility, blast_radius).

risk_score.description: Overall risk score (0.0 low to 1.0 critical).

safe.description: Whether the action is within acceptable risk thresholds.

examples.summary: Compute a numeric risk score for an action

description: Generate a structured citation for a source fragment.

locator.description: Optional source locator such as page or section.

examples.summary: Generate a citation from a source

description: Verify whether a claim is supported by provided sources.

Resolve source_ref fields in a list of corpus items to actual text content. Handles pdf_path, fs_path, url, and raw_text source types. Items that already have content are passed through unchanged. The resolved items are suitable for passing to model.output.generate or further analysis steps.

items.description: List of corpus items. Each item may have a source_ref object with type and location fields, or may already have a content string. Supported source_ref types: pdf_path, fs_path, url, raw_text, memory_key (pass-through).

Same items with content populated from source_ref where applicable. Items that already had content are unchanged. Items whose source cannot be resolved receive an empty content string and a resolution_error field.

examples.summary: Retrieve full content from source references

description: Apply policy gates to decide whether an output can be released.
Detect personally identifiable information in text content.

findings.description: Detected PII findings with types and spans.

examples.text: Email me at john.doe@example.com or call +1 650 555 1234

description: Redact personally identifiable information in text content.
Detect secret-like tokens in text content.
Compute aggregate values over table columns. Supports sum, avg, min, max, count. Can optionally group results by a key column.

table.description: Input table as a list of row objects.

aggregations.description: Aggregation operations. Each entry has: field (column name), function ("sum" | "avg" | "min" | "max" | "count").

group_by.description: Optional column name to group rows by before aggregating.

results.description: Aggregation results. If group_by is used, one result per group. Each result has the group key (if grouped) and one entry per aggregation: {field}_{function} → value.

examples.summary: Compute average salary grouped by department

description: Filter rows in tabular data according to a condition.
Sort table rows by one or more columns with specified order (ascending or descending). Supports multi-column sorting with priority.

table.description: Input table as a list of row objects.

sort_by.description: Sorting specification. Each entry is an object with: field (string), order ("asc" | "desc", defaults "asc"). First entry is primary sort.

description: Read data from a spreadsheet file (CSV, Excel, or similar tabular format). Returns the table data as an array of row objects together with column headers. For reading generic files use fs.file.read; for reading PDFs use pdf.document.read.

sheet_name.description: Name of the sheet or tab to read (for multi-sheet formats). Default: first sheet.

range.description: Cell range to read (e.g., "A1:D50"). Default: entire sheet.

table.description: Array of row objects, each with column names as keys.

headers.description: List of column header names in order.

row_count.description: Number of data rows read (excluding header).

examples.summary: Read a CSV file into structured rows

description: Write tabular data to a spreadsheet file (CSV, Excel, or similar format). Accepts an array of row objects and optional headers. For writing generic files use fs.file.write.

path.description: Destination path for the spreadsheet file.

table.description: Array of row objects to write. Each object's keys become columns.

headers.description: Explicit column order. If omitted, keys from the first row are used in natural order.

sheet_name.description: Sheet or tab name for multi-sheet formats. Default: "Sheet1".

written.description: Whether the file was successfully written.

path.description: Actual path where the file was written.

List cases, optionally filtered by status or assignee.

status_filter.description: Filter by case status (open, closed, in_progress).

Update fields of an existing case.

fields.description: Fields to update with new values.

Schedule a future event, reminder, or recurring timer. Creates a scheduled entry with a target time and optional recurrence pattern. For milestone scheduling use task.milestone.schedule; for SLA monitoring use task.sla.monitor.

title.description: Title or name of the scheduled event.

description.description: Detailed description of the event purpose.

attendees.description: List of attendee identifiers (emails, user ids) to notify.

context.description: Additional context or metadata to attach to the event.

event_id.description: Unique identifier for the created event.

scheduled.description: Whether the event was successfully scheduled.

scheduled_time.description: The confirmed scheduled time in ISO 8601.

description: Create a new incident ticket with severity and affected system information.

affected_system.description: Name or ID of the affected system.

description: Schedule a project milestone with a target date and deliverables.

safety.description: Schedules a milestone in the in-memory store.

description: Classify the priority of a task based on its attributes and context.

task.description: Task object with title, description, type, and optional urgency signals.

context.description: Contextual information such as SLA tier or impacted users.

priority.description: Classified priority: critical, high, medium, or low.

description: Monitor SLA compliance for a set of tasks and raise alerts for breaches.

tasks.description: List of tasks with created_at, priority, and status fields.

sla_rules.description: SLA rules mapping priority to max_resolution_hours.

at_risk.description: Tasks approaching SLA breach (within 80% of limit).

description: Transition a task to a new state, validating the transition is allowed.

reason.description: Explanation if the transition was rejected.

description: Classify a text into one or more predefined categories.

Compare two text blocks for semantic and structural similarity. Reports overall similarity, specific differences, and a narrative summary. For comparing code versions use code.diff.extract; for comparing records use integration.record.compare.

dimensions.description: Aspects to compare: "meaning", "structure", "tone", "detail", "completeness". Default: all dimensions.

similarity.description: Overall similarity score (0.0 = completely different, 1.0 = semantically identical).

differences.description: List of identified differences, each with a dimension, description, and severity ("minor", "moderate", "major").

summary.description: Narrative summary of how the two texts relate, highlighting key similarities and differences.

examples.summary: Compare two versions of an executive summary

inputs.text_a: Revenue grew 15% in Q3 driven by the North region.
inputs.text_b: Q3 saw 15% revenue growth, primarily from Northern markets.

differences.description: Sentence structure differs — passive vs active

outputs.summary: Both texts convey the same core message about 15% Q3 revenue growth from the North, with minor wording and structural differences.

description: Generate vector embeddings from text.

examples.summary: Generate a vector embedding for text

description: Extract relevant text content from an input text block.

Generate new text from an instruction and optional context. Use when the goal is to produce original content (drafts, descriptions, paragraphs, emails, explanations) rather than transforming existing text. For condensing existing text use text.content.summarize; for modifying style/tone of existing text use text.content.rewrite.

instruction.description: What to generate — the directive that guides content production (e.g. "Write a product description for …").

context.description: Optional background information the generator can draw from. Not the text to transform (use rewrite/summarize for that).

max_length.description: Soft upper bound on output length in characters.

inputs.instruction: Write a one-sentence description of Python.

outputs.text: Python is a versatile, high-level programming language.

description: Merge multiple text items into a single consolidated text block. Accepts an array of items with text content and produces a single string with configurable separator. Deterministic, no LLM required.

items.description: Items to merge. Each item must include a content field (string). May also include id and title which are used as section headers when include_headers is true.

separator.description: Separator between merged items. Defaults to double newline.

include_headers.description: Whether to include item titles as section headers in the merged text. Defaults to true when items have titles.

text.description: Merged text block from all items.

item_count.description: Number of items that contributed content to the merged text.

examples.summary: Merge text items into a single block

description: Produce a condensed version of text preserving key ideas.

inputs.text: Machine learning is a subset of AI that enables systems to learn from data...

outputs.summary: ML lets systems learn from data without explicit programming.

description: Render a text template using variables.

Transform existing text applying a directive — change tone, simplify, expand, formalize, or adjust style while preserving the original meaning. For producing entirely new content use text.content.generate; for condensing use text.content.summarize.

goal.description: The transformation directive — what should change about the text (e.g. "make it more formal", "simplify for a 10-year-old", "expand with examples", "change tone to friendly").

description: Translate text from one language to another.
Extract named entities from a text block.

inputs.text: John Smith works at Google in Mountain View.

description: Extract keywords from a text block.

inputs.text: Python is great for machine learning and data science.

description: Detect the language of a text.

examples.summary: Detect the language of a text

description: Extract an answer to a question from a context passage.  The response must be grounded in the provided context — the capability should not fabricate information beyond what the context contains.  For open-ended text production use text.content.generate; for condensing a passage use text.content.summarize.

context.description: The reference passage that contains the information needed to answer the question.

answer.description: The answer derived from the context.

confidence.description: Confidence score (0.0–1.0) that the answer is supported by the context.

inputs.question: What is Python?
inputs.context: Python is a high-level programming language.

description: Analyze the sentiment and emotional tone of text. Returns polarity classification, numeric score, and optional dimensional breakdown. For classifying text into custom categories use text.content.classify.

text.description: The text to analyze for sentiment.

score.description: Sentiment polarity score from -1.0 (most negative) to +1.0 (most positive), with 0.0 as neutral.

dimensions.description: Per-dimension analysis when multiple dimensions are requested. Each key is a dimension name with sub-fields for label and score.

rationale.description: Brief explanation of the sentiment assessment with supporting evidence from the text.

inputs.text: The new feature is amazing but the documentation is incomplete and confusing.

outputs.rationale: Positive toward the feature ('amazing') but negative about documentation ('incomplete', 'confusing'). Net sentiment is slightly positive.

description: Extract frames from a video.
Extract main textual content from a web page.

examples.description: Extract text from an HTML page

description: Fetch content from a web URL.

Send a generic HTTP request to an API endpoint and return the response. Supports all standard methods (GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE). For fetching web pages use web.page.fetch; for searching the web use web.source.search.

url.description: The target URL for the HTTP request.

body.description: Request body payload (for POST, PUT, PATCH). Serialized as JSON.
Parsed response body. JSON responses are parsed to objects; other content types are returned as {text: "..."}.

success.description: Whether the request returned a 2xx status code.

examples.summary: POST JSON to an external API

description: Normalize web search results into corpus item format suitable for downstream research and analysis capabilities. Converts raw search result objects (url, title, snippet) into structured corpus items with source_ref for lazy content resolution. Supports quick mode (snippet as content) and deep mode (source_ref for full page fetch).

results.description: Web search results. Each result should contain at minimum a url field. May also include title, snippet, rank, domain, date.

mode.description: Processing mode. "quick" uses snippets as content (default). "deep" leaves content empty and sets source_ref for downstream resolution via research.source.retrieve.

items.description: Normalized corpus items. Each item contains: id, title, content (populated in quick mode, empty in deep mode), source_ref (always present, type=url), type (web_page), source (original URL), metadata (original snippet, rank, domain).

examples.description: Normalize search results in quick mode

description: Search the web for sources matching a query.
Verify trust and integrity signals for a web source URL.

outputs.reason: Public HTTPS source with valid domain.
